21.correct: Anita Desai's children's novel 'The Village by the Sea' won the Guardian Children's Fiction Prize.

wrong.B: 'Games at Twilight' is a short-story collection.
wrong.C: 'Diamond Dust and Other Stories' is a story collection.
wrong.D: 'Where Shall We Go This Summer?' is an adult novel.

21.fact: 'The Village by the Sea' (1982) is set in a coastal village near Mumbai.

22.correct: 'The Prelude' is William Wordsworth's long autobiographical poem.

wrong.A: 'The Excursion' is a separate long poem.
wrong.B: 'The Recluse' was a planned larger work (largely unfinished).

22.fact: 'The Prelude' traces the growth of the poet's own mind.

23.correct: Laterite soil is rich in iron (and aluminium) but poor in nitrogen and humus.

wrong.A: Alluvial soil is fertile and not iron-rich in this way.
wrong.C: Desert soil is sandy and saline.
wrong.D: Black soil is rich in lime, iron and magnesia but is moisture-retentive clay.

23.fact: Laterite soil forms in areas of high temperature and heavy rainfall.

24.correct: In recent happiness reports, the Nordic countries (northern Europe) consistently score the highest.

wrong.A: Sub-Saharan Africa scores low on the index.
wrong.B: The Middle East does not top the index.

24.fact: Finland has topped the World Happiness Report for several years running.

25.correct: The IAAF renamed itself 'World Athletics' in 2019.
25.fact: World Athletics is the global governing body for track and field, road running and race walking.

26.correct: BIMSTEC traces its origin to the Bangkok Declaration of 1997, which set up the sub-regional grouping.

wrong.A: The Kathmandu Declaration is not its founding declaration.
wrong.B: The Colombo Declaration is not it.
wrong.D: The Dhaka Declaration is not the founding one.

26.fact: BIMSTEC links Bay-of-Bengal nations: India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Myanmar, Thailand, Nepal and Bhutan.

27.correct: Hormones like adrenaline belong to the endocrine system, which secretes hormones into the blood.

wrong.A: The nervous system uses electrical impulses, though it interacts with the endocrine system.

27.fact: Adrenaline (epinephrine) is released by the adrenal glands during the 'fight or flight' response.

28.correct: The main aim of Ashoka's 'Dhamma' was to encourage moral living and social harmony among his subjects.

wrong.A: It was not to impose Buddhism as a state religion.
wrong.B: It was not about administrative centralisation.
wrong.D: It was not about foreign alliances.

28.fact: Ashoka spread his Dhamma through rock and pillar edicts across his empire.

29.correct: The phrase 'Hindu rate of growth' was coined by the economist Raj Krishna.

wrong.A: Montek Singh Ahluwalia did not coin it.
wrong.C: Jagdish Bhagwati did not coin it.
wrong.D: Amartya Sen did not coin it.

29.fact: The term described India's low (~3-4%) growth rate from the 1950s to the 1980s.

30.correct: India uses the Combined Consumer Price Index (CPI) to measure headline (retail) inflation for monetary-policy targeting.

wrong.B: The GDP deflator is a broad measure but not the headline inflation gauge used.
wrong.C: The Index of Industrial Production measures output, not inflation.
wrong.D: The Wholesale Price Index measures wholesale, not retail, prices.

30.fact: The RBI targets CPI inflation at 4% (with a band of +/- 2%).

31.correct: The traveller Ibn Battuta gave a clear account of Muhammad bin Tughlaq and his reign.

wrong.B: Marco Polo travelled mainly to China and southern India earlier.
wrong.C: Al-Biruni came with Mahmud of Ghazni, before the Tughlaqs.
wrong.D: Domingo Paes described Vijayanagara, not the Tughlaqs.

31.fact: Ibn Battuta, a Moroccan traveller, served as a qazi (judge) in Delhi under Muhammad bin Tughlaq.

32.correct: Neither statement is correct - separation of the judiciary from the executive is Article 50 (not 47), and promotion of international peace is Article 51 (not 50). So 'Neither I nor II'.

wrong.A: 'Only I' is wrong as the article is misidentified.
wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong as the article is misidentified.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since both are misidentified.

32.fact: Article 47 deals with raising the level of nutrition and improving public health.

33.correct: Since the launch of the Jal Jeevan Mission in 2019, over 15 crore rural households had been provided tap-water connections by July 2024.

wrong.B: 21 crore overstates it for that date.

33.fact: The Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide functional tap-water connections to all rural households.

34.correct: The DAJGUA tribal-development campaign includes both healthcare (I) and education (II) components, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are components.

34.fact: The campaign aims to saturate tribal villages with multiple basic services.

35.correct: The election to fill a vacancy in the office of the President must be held before the expiry of six months from the date the vacancy occurs.

wrong.C: 4 months is not the constitutional limit.

35.fact: The person elected then serves a full five-year term.

36.correct: Rajasthan's 'Gangaur' festival is celebrated in honour of Goddess Parvati (Gauri).

wrong.A: It is not in honour of Goddess Lakshmi.
wrong.B: It is not for Goddess Kali.
wrong.D: It is not for Goddess Saraswati.

36.fact: Gangaur celebrates marital love and the bond between Shiva and Parvati.

37.correct: The first fully successful flight of India's Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle was PSLV-D2 (1994).

wrong.B: PSLV-C5 was a later operational flight.

37.fact: The PSLV became ISRO's reliable workhorse after its early developmental flights.

38.correct: The statement 'Evaporation causes heating' is NOT correct - evaporation actually causes cooling (it absorbs latent heat).

wrong.B: It is true that states of matter are interconvertible.
wrong.C: It is true that boiling is a bulk phenomenon.
wrong.D: It is true that evaporation is a surface phenomenon.

38.fact: Sweating cools the body because the evaporating sweat absorbs heat from the skin.

39.correct: Mediterranean agriculture is a highly specialised commercial type of farming (e.g. citrus fruits, grapes, olives).
39.fact: Mediterranean regions are famous for viticulture (grape-growing for wine) and horticulture.

40.correct: Basketball is believed to have started in India around 1905, at the YMCA in Calcutta.

wrong.D: 1935 is when the Basketball Federation of India was formed.

40.fact: The YMCA played a key role in introducing basketball and volleyball to India.
